elections.title: U.S. Presidential Election 2026
elections.system: Electoral College (538 electors, 270 needed to win)

timeline.label: Primary Elections
timeline.description: Voters in each state choose their party's presidential nominee.

timeline.label: Party Conventions
timeline.description: Official nomination of candidates. Running mates (VP candidates) announced.

timeline.label: Presidential Debates
timeline.description: Major national debates televised to help voters compare candidates.

timeline.label: Early & Mail-In Voting
timeline.description: Many states allow voting before Election Day by mail or in-person at early voting centers.

timeline.label: Election Day
timeline.description: First Tuesday after first Monday in November. Voters elect 538 Electors (not directly the President).

timeline.label: Electoral College Vote
timeline.description: Electors in each state cast their official votes. 270 electoral votes needed to win.

timeline.label: Congressional Certification
timeline.description: Congress certifies the Electoral College results.

timeline.label: Inauguration
timeline.description: President takes the oath of office on the steps of the Capitol Building.

elections.title: UK General Election 2026
elections.system: First Past the Post — 650 seats in House of Commons

timeline.label: Parliament Dissolved
timeline.description: Prime Minister calls election. Parliament dissolves and MPs stop being MPs.

timeline.label: Campaign Period
timeline.description: 5-week campaign where parties make promises. Manifestos published.

timeline.label: Polling Day
timeline.description: Voters go to polling stations. Paper ballots are used, not electronic machines.

timeline.label: Overnight Count
timeline.description: Paper ballots counted through the night. Results announced constituency by constituency.

timeline.label: New PM Invited
timeline.description: King invites the leader of the majority party to Buckingham Palace to form a government.

elections.title: European Parliament Election 2026
elections.system: Proportional Representation — 720 seats across 27 member states

timeline.label: European Campaign Period
timeline.description: Political groups across all 27 EU countries campaign simultaneously.

timeline.label: Election Days (4 days)
timeline.description: Different EU countries vote on different days. Citizens vote for national party lists.

timeline.label: Results Published
timeline.description: Results published after all countries have closed polls to prevent influence.

timeline.label: New Parliament Constituted
timeline.description: New MEPs take their seats. Political groups form coalitions to elect Parliament president.

systems.name: First Past the Post (FPTP)
systems.description: Candidate with most votes wins, even without majority. Simple but can ignore minority votes.

systems.name: Proportional Representation (PR)
systems.description: Seats distributed based on total vote percentage. Better minority representation.

systems.name: Mixed Member PR
systems.description: Combines FPTP constituency seats with PR top-up seats.

systems.name: Two-Round System
systems.description: If no candidate gets 50%+, top two candidates compete in a runoff round.
